name: Orb / Sphere
description: A perfectly spherical luminous object, 0.5–6 feet in diameter. May appear as a solid metallic sphere, a translucent glowing ball, or a plasma-like energetic form. The defining modern sensor-era UAP alongside the Tic Tac. Transmedium capable — documented entering and exiting water without splash or disturbance. Often appears in groups. IR signature is complex: some appear warmer than ambient, some cooler, some thermally neutral.

ir_signature.ir_notes: USS Omaha footage: 6-foot sphere, illuminated, solid mass confirmed by three-sensor radar correlation. Appears bright (hot) in WHT HOT mode. Descends into Pacific with zero surface disturbance.

cultural_context: The orb/sphere class has become the most commonly reported UAP type in the post-2017 disclosure era. Ryan Graves (former F/A-18 pilot) testified before Congress in July 2023 that his squadron encountered solid spheres near the Virginia coast for years. The transmedium characteristic — smooth water entry and exit — is the defining behavior. Related to but distinct from plasma orbs, ball lightning, and atmospheric phenomena.
